_about: Data for the 'Make a Drink' discovery flow. Each cocktail is written ONCE as a base spirit + an unordered SET of ingredients. The app derives the whole decision tree from this: after the base it walks the category order below (defining → finishing), only ever offering choices that keep a real recipe reachable, auto-folds any forced single step, and reveals what you've made so you can add it to your order. Add a cocktail = add one entry here. Variants (Gibson, Dirty Martini, Tommy's…) are just real recipes that differ by an ingredient or finish.

categoryLabels.herb: A herb, muddle or cooling note?

cocktails.name: Gin & Tonic
cocktails.blurb: The effortless classic — crisp, bitter, endlessly refreshing.

cocktails.name: Negroni
cocktails.blurb: Equal parts, beautifully bitter. The aperitivo benchmark.

cocktails.name: Dry Martini
cocktails.blurb: Cold, clean, ceremonial. Gin's purest stage.

cocktails.name: Gibson
cocktails.blurb: A Martini that swapped the olive for a tangy onion. Savoury and sharp.

cocktails.name: Martini, with a Twist
cocktails.blurb: The same icy Martini, finished with a citrus-oil snap of lemon.

cocktails.name: Dirty Martini
cocktails.blurb: A splash of brine makes it cloudy, salty and dangerously moreish.

cocktails.name: Tom Collins
cocktails.blurb: A gin sour lengthened with soda — tall and sparkling.

cocktails.name: Gin Fizz
cocktails.blurb: A Tom Collins gone silky — egg white gives a cloud-soft head.

cocktails.name: Gimlet
cocktails.blurb: Gin and lime, sharp and direct. Nothing to hide behind.

cocktails.name: Aviation
cocktails.blurb: A pale violet pre-Prohibition beauty.

cocktails.name: French 75
cocktails.blurb: Gin sour with a champagne crown. Dangerously elegant.

cocktails.name: Bramble
cocktails.blurb: Gin sour drizzled with blackberry liqueur. Late-summer in a glass.

cocktails.name: Southside
cocktails.blurb: A minty gin sour — like a Mojito's dressed-up cousin.

cocktails.name: Last Word
cocktails.blurb: Equal parts and herbaceous. Complex, balanced, unforgettable.

cocktails.name: Elderflower Collins
cocktails.blurb: Floral, fragrant, garden-party perfect.

cocktails.name: White Lady
cocktails.blurb: A gin Sidecar with a silky egg-white finish.

cocktails.name: Clover Club
cocktails.blurb: Pink, frothy and pre-Prohibition pretty. Raspberry and lemon.

cocktails.name: Vesper
cocktails.blurb: Bond's own: gin, vodka and Lillet. 'Shaken, not stirred.'

cocktails.name: Corpse Reviver No. 2
cocktails.blurb: A hair-of-the-dog legend with an absinthe whisper.

cocktails.name: Singapore Sling
cocktails.blurb: The Raffles Hotel classic — fruity, spiced and ruby-red.

cocktails.name: Bee's Knees
cocktails.blurb: Prohibition's honeyed cover-up. Bright, golden, gone too soon.

cocktails.name: Pink Gin
cocktails.blurb: Navy-strength gin barely tinged with bitters. Spare and steely.

cocktails.name: Sloe Gin Fizz
cocktails.blurb: Tart sloe berries lengthened into a blushing fizz.

cocktails.name: Martinez
cocktails.blurb: The Martini's sweeter Victorian ancestor. Rich and aromatic.

cocktails.name: Moscow Mule
cocktails.blurb: Spicy, zesty, fizzy. Best served properly cold.

cocktails.name: Cosmopolitan
cocktails.blurb: Tart, pink and iconic. The '90s never tasted so good.

cocktails.name: Espresso Martini
cocktails.blurb: The pick-me-up that picks you back up. Velvety crema essential.

cocktails.name: Vodka Martini
cocktails.blurb: Clean and bracing. Shaken if you're feeling Bond.

cocktails.name: Bloody Mary
cocktails.blurb: Savoury, spicy, brunch's medicine cabinet.

cocktails.name: Screwdriver
cocktails.blurb: Two ingredients, zero fuss. Breakfast's guilty pleasure.

cocktails.name: Sea Breeze
cocktails.blurb: Cranberry and grapefruit — tart, coastal, easy-going.

cocktails.name: Bay Breeze
cocktails.blurb: The Sea Breeze gone tropical — cranberry meets pineapple.

cocktails.name: Madras
cocktails.blurb: Cranberry and orange juice, sunset-hued and gulpable.

cocktails.name: White Russian
cocktails.blurb: Coffee, cream, vodka. Dessert that abides.

cocktails.name: Black Russian
cocktails.blurb: The White Russian before the cream. Dark and simple.

cocktails.name: Mudslide
cocktails.blurb: An adults-only milkshake — coffee, Irish cream and a sweet tooth.

cocktails.name: Cucumber Cooler
cocktails.blurb: Crisp, clean and spa-day refreshing.

cocktails.name: Vodka Tonic
cocktails.blurb: Cleaner than a G&T, and just as easy.

cocktails.name: Vodka Collins
cocktails.blurb: The Tom Collins, gone neutral. Tall, lemony, fizzy.

cocktails.name: Lemon Drop
cocktails.blurb: Like sherbet for grown-ups — tart, sweet, sunshine-bright.

cocktails.name: Kamikaze
cocktails.blurb: Three-ingredient bar-room dynamite. Tart and to the point.

cocktails.name: Harvey Wallbanger
cocktails.blurb: A Screwdriver with a vanilla-herbal Galliano float. Very 1970s.

cocktails.name: Greyhound
cocktails.blurb: Vodka and grapefruit. Bracingly bitter, beautifully plain.

cocktails.name: Salty Dog
cocktails.blurb: A Greyhound that rolled in salt — savoury edge, tart heart.

cocktails.name: Margarita
cocktails.blurb: Tequila's sharpest, brightest classic.

cocktails.name: Tommy's Margarita
cocktails.blurb: No orange liqueur — just tequila, lime and agave. Purist's choice.

cocktails.name: Spicy Margarita
cocktails.blurb: The Margarita with a kick that lingers.

cocktails.name: Cadillac Margarita
cocktails.blurb: The Margarita in first class — a Grand Marnier float on top.

cocktails.name: Strawberry Margarita
cocktails.blurb: Summer in a frosty glass — ripe strawberry over bright lime.

cocktails.name: Paloma
cocktails.blurb: Mexico's everyday favourite — bubbly, tart, sunny.

cocktails.name: Tequila Sunrise
cocktails.blurb: Let the grenadine sink for that sunrise gradient.

cocktails.name: Ranch Water
cocktails.blurb: West Texas minimalism — tequila, lime, sparkling water. That's it.

cocktails.name: Tequila Sour
cocktails.blurb: A silky agave sour with a soft foam crown.

cocktails.name: El Diablo
cocktails.blurb: Tequila, blackcurrant and ginger — a devilishly good highball.

cocktails.name: Matador
cocktails.blurb: Pineapple and lime over tequila — easy, tropical, breezy.

cocktails.name: Tequila Old Fashioned
cocktails.blurb: Añejo tequila stirred down like an Old Fashioned. Smooth and warming.

cocktails.name: Brave Bull
cocktails.blurb: A Black Russian's tequila cousin — coffee liqueur, agave bite.

cocktails.name: Daiquiri
cocktails.blurb: Three ingredients in perfect tension. The bartender's litmus test.

cocktails.name: Mojito
cocktails.blurb: Muddled mint and lime, lengthened with soda. Havana in a glass.

cocktails.name: Piña Colada
cocktails.blurb: If you like getting caught in the rain.

cocktails.name: Dark 'n' Stormy
cocktails.blurb: Dark rum floated over spicy ginger beer. Brooding and brilliant.

cocktails.name: Cuba Libre
cocktails.blurb: Rum and coke, but the lime makes it a cocktail. Honest.

cocktails.name: Mai Tai
cocktails.blurb: The tiki titan — nutty orgeat, orange and lime.

cocktails.name: Hurricane
cocktails.blurb: New Orleans' fruity, ruby-red storm.

cocktails.name: Rum Punch
cocktails.blurb: 'One of sour, two of sweet…' — the Caribbean's house party in a cup.

cocktails.name: Painkiller
cocktails.blurb: A Piña Colada with orange and a grate of nutmeg. Soothing stuff.

cocktails.name: Zombie
cocktails.blurb: Don Beach's overproof tiki bomb. Famously limited to two.

cocktails.name: Hemingway Daiquiri
cocktails.blurb: Papa's sugarless Daiquiri — grapefruit, maraschino, no sweetness.

cocktails.name: Rum Old Fashioned
cocktails.blurb: Aged rum stirred with rich demerara. Molasses-dark and mellow.

cocktails.name: Bacardí Cocktail
cocktails.blurb: A Daiquiri blushing pink with grenadine — by law, Bacardí rum.

cocktails.name: Jungle Bird
cocktails.blurb: Bitter Campari meets tropical pineapple. The thinking drinker's tiki.

cocktails.name: Mary Pickford
cocktails.blurb: A silent-film-era charmer — pineapple, cherry and a rosy glow.

cocktails.name: Planter's Punch
cocktails.blurb: Jamaica's tall, fruity, bitters-laced afternoon refresher.

cocktails.name: Old Fashioned
cocktails.blurb: Spirit, sugar, bitters, ice. The original cocktail, undefeated.

cocktails.name: Whiskey Sour
cocktails.blurb: Tart, silky, balanced. Egg white optional but luxurious.

cocktails.name: Manhattan
cocktails.blurb: Rye, vermouth, bitters. Sophistication, stirred.

cocktails.name: Dry Manhattan
cocktails.blurb: The Manhattan gone crisp — dry vermouth, a paler, sharper take.

cocktails.name: Perfect Manhattan
cocktails.blurb: Split sweet and dry vermouth for a balanced 'perfect' middle.

cocktails.name: Boulevardier
cocktails.blurb: A Negroni in a whiskey coat. Warmer, deeper, bitter.

cocktails.name: Mint Julep
cocktails.blurb: Bourbon and mint over a frosty mountain of crushed ice. Derby day.

cocktails.name: Whiskey Smash
cocktails.blurb: A julep crossed with a sour — bourbon, lemon and a fistful of mint.

cocktails.name: Sazerac
cocktails.blurb: An absinthe-rinsed New Orleans legend. Aromatic and bold.

cocktails.name: Whiskey Highball
cocktails.blurb: Whiskey and soda, ice-cold. The Japanese art of less.

cocktails.name: Gold Rush
cocktails.blurb: A modern classic: bourbon, honey, lemon. Three-ingredient gold.

cocktails.name: Paper Plane
cocktails.blurb: Equal parts bourbon, Aperol, amaro and lemon. A modern equal-parts marvel.

cocktails.name: New York Sour
cocktails.blurb: A Whiskey Sour crowned with a dramatic float of red wine.

cocktails.name: Vieux Carré
cocktails.blurb: The French Quarter in a glass — rye, cognac and herbal Bénédictine.

cocktails.name: Brown Derby
cocktails.blurb: A Hollywood throwback — bourbon, grapefruit and honey.

cocktails.name: Lynchburg Lemonade
cocktails.blurb: Tennessee whiskey lengthened into a long, fizzy lemonade.

cocktails.name: Irish Coffee
cocktails.blurb: Hot coffee, Irish whiskey and a soft collar of cream. Pure comfort.

cocktails.name: Rob Roy
cocktails.blurb: A Manhattan made with Scotch — smoky, stirred, dignified.

cocktails.name: Rusty Nail
cocktails.blurb: Scotch sweetened with honeyed, herbal Drambuie. Two ingredients, deep warmth.

cocktails.name: Blood and Sand
cocktails.blurb: The only Scotch classic with orange juice — and it works. Named for a bullfight.

cocktails.name: Penicillin
cocktails.blurb: A modern cure-all: blended Scotch, honey, ginger and a smoky float.

cocktails.name: Bobby Burns

cocktails.name: Godfather

cocktails.name: Sidecar

cocktails.name: Brandy Alexander
cocktails.blurb: Chocolatey, creamy, after-dinner indulgence.

cocktails.name: Brandy Old Fashioned

cocktails.name: French Connection

cocktails.name: Stinger
cocktails.blurb: Cognac and white crème de menthe. A cool, minty nightcap.

cocktails.name: Brandy Sour
cocktails.blurb: The simplest brandy sour — tart, balanced, timeless.

cocktails.name: Horse's Neck

cocktails.name: Mezcal Margarita

cocktails.name: Naked & Famous
cocktails.blurb: Equal parts smoke, herb, bitter and citrus. A modern equal-parts marvel.

cocktails.name: Mezcal Paloma

cocktails.name: Mezcal Negroni

cocktails.name: Pisco Punch

cocktails.name: Mimosa

cocktails.name: Bellini

cocktails.name: Tuxedo No. 2

cocktails.name: Hanky Panky

cocktails.name: Gin Rickey

cocktails.name: Sherry Cobbler

cocktails.name: Fino Highball

cocktails.blurb: A French 75 with vodka in the cockpit.

cocktails.name: Seelbach
cocktails.blurb: A bitters-heavy bourbon-and-bubbles showpiece.

cocktails.name: Death in the Afternoon
cocktails.blurb: Hemingway's louche, anise-clouded bubbly.

cocktails.name: Chrysanthemum
cocktails.blurb: A vermouth-forward, herbal aperitif.

cocktails.name: Trident
cocktails.blurb: A Negroni riff for the Nordic soul.

cocktails.name: Aquavit Sour
cocktails.blurb: A caraway-scented Scandinavian sour.

cocktails.name: Oslo Mule
cocktails.blurb: The Mule, gone Scandinavian.

cocktails.name: Saketini
cocktails.blurb: A clean, delicate Martini riff with sake.

cocktails.name: Nigori Colada
cocktails.blurb: A cloudy-sake take on the Piña Colada.

cocktails.blurb: A lengthened, sessionable absinthe punch.

cocktails.name: Absinthe Frappé

cocktails.name: Holland Gin Fizz
